cards.front: What is the goal of image restoration in the presence of noise only?
cards.back: To remove or minimize the effects of noise from an image and reveal the original or clean image

cards.front: What type of noise can cause image degradation in the presence of noise only?
cards.back: Gaussian noise, shot noise, thermal noise, and other types of random noise

cards.front: Which algorithm is commonly used for image restoration in the presence of noise only?
cards.back: Wiener filter, Bayesian methods, and other statistical methods

cards.front: What is the key challenge in image restoration in the presence of noise only?
cards.back: Separating the original signal from the noise, which can be difficult due to the limited number of independent measurements

cards.front: Can image restoration in the presence of noise only always restore the original image?
cards.back: No, as some noise types can be irreversible and cannot be completely removed from the image
